title: Olearia angustata
intro: Olearia angustata, also known as New Zealand tree daisy, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its shrubby habit and white or pink flowers. The leaves of Olearia angustata contain various bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, providing potential health benefits such as wound healing, immune system support, and protection against oxidative stress. These bioactive compounds have been identified as quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, among others, contributing to the plant's medicinal properties. Traditionally, the leaves of Olearia angustata are used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
